metrics.name: Fulfilled profitable customer outcome
metrics.definition: A customer outcome that is accepted by operations, fulfilled, and eligible for revenue or contribution analysis after cancellations, failed payments, refunds, returns, no-shows, and support issues are accounted for.
metrics.formula: owner-approved fulfilled customer outcomes with revenue-quality eligibility after exclusions
metrics.source: bookings, orders, payment_events, invoice_events, customer/account history, and approved analytics platform
metrics.privacy_note: Requires owner-approved status definitions and privacy-safe reconciliation. Do not infer from clicks, booking starts, checkout success views, or platform conversions alone.

metrics.name: Organic qualified visit count
metrics.definition: Consent-aware visits or Search Console clicks landing on qualifying public routes.
metrics.formula: count of qualifying analytics visits or Search Console clicks by route/date; grains must remain separate
metrics.source: approved analytics platform plus Google Search Console
metrics.privacy_note: Search Console clicks and analytics sessions are different grains and must not be merged as identical counts.

metrics.name: Service booking start count
metrics.definition: Visitor starts a public booking journey from a service, product install, or related entry point.
metrics.privacy_note: A start is intent, not a submitted or accepted booking.

metrics.name: Server accepted booking count
metrics.definition: Bookings with a server booking id that are eligible under owner-approved submitted or accepted rules.
metrics.formula: count(distinct booking_id) where booking insert/function succeeds and status is eligible under owner-approved rules
metrics.privacy_note: Confirmed booking is not completed service and may later cancel or no-show.

metrics.name: Completed service count
metrics.definition: Bookings where owner-approved operational status indicates completed service.
metrics.formula: count(distinct booking_id) where owner-approved status indicates completed service
metrics.privacy_note: Requires operational status definition and cannot be inferred from analytics.

metrics.name: Product view count
metrics.definition: Product detail page renders a valid product for a visitor.
metrics.source: approved analytics events and catalog source
metrics.privacy_note: Product view does not prove product fit, stock, cart, or purchase.

metrics.name: Checkout started count
metrics.definition: Checkout or payment-creation flow starts after cart and client validation state allows progression.
metrics.privacy_note: Checkout start is not payment success or fulfilled order.

metrics.name: Paid order count
metrics.definition: Orders where signed Paytrail callback or approved payment source confirms paid status under owner-approved policy.
metrics.formula: count(distinct order_id) where order/payment status is owner-approved paid status and signature/payment validation passes

metrics.name: Fulfilled order count
metrics.definition: Orders where fulfillment status is owner-approved fulfilled, picked-up, or delivered.
metrics.formula: count(distinct order_id) where fulfillment_status is owner-approved fulfilled/picked-up/delivered status
metrics.privacy_note: Paid order may still refund, return, fail stock, or fail fulfillment.

metrics.name: Local contact action count
metrics.definition: User activates public phone, email, or directions actions.
metrics.source: approved analytics events and Google Business Profile actions where available
metrics.privacy_note: Clicking phone, email, or directions is not a customer outcome.

metrics.name: Retention repeat customer count
metrics.definition: Customers with repeat booking/order or owner-approved retention milestone within a cohort window.
metrics.formula: count(distinct customer_id) with repeat booking/order or owner-approved retention milestone within cohort window
metrics.source: customer account, bookings, orders, Tire Hotel/storage, service history
metrics.privacy_note: Requires customer identity rules and privacy-safe cohort treatment.

metrics.name: Revenue EUR
metrics.definition: Owner-approved recognized revenue in EUR under approved treatment for VAT, fees, refunds, returns, and manual/cash flows.
metrics.formula: sum(owner-approved recognized revenue in EUR excluding or including tax/fees only according to approved finance definition)
metrics.privacy_note: Do not report as audited revenue until recognition, VAT, refunds, fees, and reconciliation are approved.

metrics.name: Gross or contribution profit EUR
metrics.definition: Revenue minus approved cost of goods/service and approved variable fulfillment, payment, and support costs.
metrics.formula: revenue_eur - approved cost_of_goods_or_service - approved variable fulfillment/payment/support costs
metrics.privacy_note: Margin cannot be inferred from website or payment events alone.

metrics.name: Cancellation refund return rate
metrics.definition: Cancelled bookings plus refunded or returned orders divided by server-accepted outcomes.
metrics.privacy_note: Guardrail must move with primary outcome analysis; do not optimize starts if this worsens.

metrics.name: Measurement privacy failure count
metrics.definition: Events, logs, reports, or exports containing prohibited PII or firing outside approved consent behavior.
metrics.formula: count of privacy, consent, or prohibited-parameter incidents
metrics.source: QA, code review, analytics debug, privacy review
metrics.privacy_note: Any material privacy failure blocks growth-ready measurement.

metrics.name: Critical journey accessibility failure count
metrics.definition: Blocker or critical accessibility failures in booking, checkout, contact, account, and recovery journeys.
metrics.formula: count of blocker/critical accessibility failures in critical journeys
metrics.privacy_note: Conversion work cannot ship if it prevents task completion.

relationships.note: Qualified acquisition is necessary but not sufficient for fulfilled value.
Booking starts can contribute to submitted bookings only after form and server acceptance.
Server-accepted booking is an upstream state for completed service.
Completed service is a candidate fulfilled customer outcome after exclusions.
Product views diagnose product decision engagement but do not prove purchase intent.
Checkout start can lead to paid order only after server/payment success.
Paid order is upstream of fulfillment but can still refund, return, or fail fulfillment.
Fulfilled order is a candidate fulfilled customer outcome after exclusions.
Contact actions may contribute to accepted bookings when operationally knowable.
Repeat/retention outcomes can indicate durable customer value after definitions are approved.
Revenue must be interpreted with fulfilled value and exclusions.
Profit/margin prevents optimizing gross revenue that harms business quality.
Growth that increases cancellations, refunds, or returns is not healthy.
Privacy or consent failure blocks trustworthy growth measurement.
Critical journey accessibility failure invalidates conversion-quality claims.
